reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. `[Field K]` together with `[Fintype Kˣ]` is equivalent to "K is a finite field" since in a field K = Kˣ ⊔ {0}, so K is finite iff Kˣ is finite — this is the standard Mathlib encoding (cf. `prod_univ_units_id_eq_neg_one` in `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finite/Basic.lean`, which uses the same hypothesis form). The nonzero elements of a field are exactly its units, so `∏ x : Kˣ, x` is the product of nonzero elements, and the equality `= -1` in Kˣ is equivalent to the product being -1 in K via the canonical unit embedding. No hypothesis is dropped or added that changes the mathematical content.
